steps.title: Step 1 — the starting expression
steps.body: We want to simplify $A \cdot B + A \cdot \overline{B}$. Both terms contain a factor of $A$, which is the key to simplifying.

steps.title: Step 2 — factor out the common term
steps.body:
Apply the **distributive law** in reverse to pull out the shared $A$:

$$A \cdot B + A \cdot \overline{B} = A \cdot (B + \overline{B}).$$

steps.title: Step 3 — apply the complement law
steps.body:
A variable ORed with its own complement is always true: $B + \overline{B} = 1$. So the expression becomes

$$A \cdot (B + \overline{B}) = A \cdot 1.$$

steps.title: Step 4 — apply the identity law
steps.body:
Anything ANDed with $1$ is itself: $A \cdot 1 = A$. The whole expression simplifies to just

$$A.$$

Two AND gates and an OR gate collapse into a single wire.
